State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


May 11, 1983


James Dilworth
Attorney at Law
7172 Magnolia Avenue
Riverside, CA  92504

Re:  Advice No. A-83-118

Dear Mr. Dilworth:

     You have asked our advice on behalf of Margaret C. Wright,
one of the elected directors of the San Bernardino Valley
Municipal Water District ("District"), with respect to her
participation in the proposed letting of a contract for the
construction of the Santa Ana River Crossing ("SARC") Pipeline.
Our advice is based on the following facts supplied in your
letter seeking advice and in our subsequent telephone
conversation.

     The SARC pipeline, which the District is planning to
construct, will cross property owned by the North Fork Water
Company.  The North Fork property is in a floodplain, and is
presently used for provision of irrigation water to its
stockholders.  North Fork has no plans to change the use of this
property.  The SARC pipeline will cross the property, but will
not be used for, or available for use for, supplying water to
the property now served by North Fork.  The SARC pipeline will
not affect the supply of water to the North Fork property.  The
pipeline will cross the North Fork property along an easement
which has already been obtained by the District.  As far as you
know, the construction of the pipeline over the North Fork
property will not affect the value of that property.

     Mrs. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of
over $1,000 in North Fork.  In addition, her husband is an
officer of North Fork.  Mrs. Wright disqualified herself from
participating in the District vote on the easement over the
North Fork property.  She has now asked whether she can
participate in future decisions concerning the contract for the
SARC pipeline.

James Dilworth
May 11, 1983
Page 2


     The Political Reform Act prohibits an official from making
or participating in a governmental decision in which she has a
financial interest. Government Code Section 87100.2/  An
official has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on a business entity in which the official has an
investment of over $1,000. Section 87103. Since Ms. Wright
and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in
North Fork, she must disqualify herself from participating in
any decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have a material financial effect on North Fork.

     From the facts that you have provided, it does not appear
that the construction of the SARC pipeline over the North Fork
property will have a material financial effect on the value of
North Fork's property,2/ or on its income3/ or other
assets. Therefore, it is our advice that the Political Reform
Act does not require Ms. Wright to disqualify herself from
decisions on the contract for construction of the pipeline.

     This letter does not constitute advice under Section 1090.
Since Ms. Wright and her husband own over 3% of the stock of
North Fork, and her husband is an officer of North Fork (see
Section 1091.5), you may want to consider the possible
application of Section 1090 to this situation.

_______________________

     1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise stated.

     2/ If any facts come to light which suggest that the
pipeline will affect the value of the property, you should
consult Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 to
determine whether the effect would be material within the
meaning of the Act.

     3/ If any facts come to light which suggest that either
the contract under consideration or the pipeline will affect
North Fork's income, you should consult Commission regulation
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 to determine whether the effect
would be material within the meaning of the Act.

James Dilworth
May 11, 1983
Page 3


     I hope this has answered your questions. If you want to
discuss this matter further, please feel free to call me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   Barbara A. Milman
                                   Chief
                                   Legal Division

BAM:pln

JAMES W. DILWORTH
Attorney at Law
7172 Magnolia Avenue
Riverside, CA 92504

April 22, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: San Bernardino Valley Municipal Water District

Gentlemen:

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114, advice and an
opinion is requested with respect to the duties of Margaret C.
Wright, one of the elected directors of the San Bernardino Valley
Municipal Water District ("District"), with respect to the
proposed letting of a contract for the construction of the Santa
Ana River Crossing ("SARC") Pipeline. A question has been raised
as to whether Mrs. Wright can vote on the award of the contract
pursuant to the competitive bids or must disqualify herself on
the question. Mrs. Wright has requested me, as attorney for the
District, to obtain appropriate advice.

Mrs. Wright was first elected to the Board of the District
in 1982 and assumed office in January, 1983. She has no
financial interest in any of the bidders on the proposed
contract. She does, however, together with her husband, have a
seven or eight percent interest as a stockholder in the North
Fork Water Company, which owns land which would be crossed by the
SARC Pipeline.

The North Fork Water Company is a mutual water company
organized under the General Corporation Law of California for the
purpose of serving water to its stockholders at cost. The water
is served for irrigation purposes, principally on citrus groves.
Mrs. Wright's husband is the President of the Board of Directors
of the North Fork Water Company. The District has a contract
with numerous agencies and companies including the North Fork
Water Company known as the Santa Ana River-Mill Creek Cooperative
Water Project Agreement which provides for provision of
supplemental water by the District and exchange of water between
the various parties so as to minimize the cost of construction of
new facilities and to maximize the availability of supplemental
water in the area of the District affected. Under this contract
the District is obliged, to the extent possible, to construct
additional facilities, including the SARC Pipeline, to complete
the facilities projected for water service under the Cooperative
Water Project Agreement. The District has previously constructed
facilities for service to the North Fork Water Company including
an interconnection with its facilities, such having been
completed in 1978.

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 22, 1983
Page Two

The proposed SARC Pipeline would cross property owned by the
North Fork Water Company. The property in question is located in
the wash area where the Santa Ana River discharges from the
mountains and is subject to easements in favor of the San
Bernardino Valley Water Conservation District ("Conservation
District"), a public agency engaged in the conservation of water
by sinking it in ponds and spreading basins so as to recharge the
underground supply of water from which persons with wells in the
San Bernardino basin pump. These easements generally authorize
the Conservation District to spread water, construct, maintain,
operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, trenches, canals and
dam and to maintain and use roadways over the property and would
appear to substantially restrict the useability of the property
for any other purpose, and the North Fork Water Company has no
present plans to use the property for any other purpose.

Mrs. Wright's husband is one of seven elected directors of
the Conservation District.

The North Fork Water Company property which will be crossed
by the SARC Pipeline already has access to water service, should
such ever be desired, from the District's foothill pipeline. The
SARC Pipeline will connect to the foothill pipeline and carry
water across the Santa Ana River Wash to facilities on the other
side. The SARC Pipeline is to be 42 inches in diameter and is a
wholesale transmission line from which service to the property
would not be available, except possibly in the vicinity of
Greenspot Road where the SARC Pipeline will connect with the
District's existing foothill pipeline, from which service is
already available.

The District has already acquired an easement over the North
Fork Water Company property in question for the SARC Pipeline.
The North Fork Water Company made no charge for the easement.
Mrs. Wright disqualified herself from participating at the time
the Board of the District voted to accept the easement in
question and disclosed her interest in the North Fork Water
Company, all of which was appropriately reflected in the
District's Minutes.

The question presented is whether Mrs. Wright's interest in
the North Fork Water Company requires her to disqualify herself
in awarding a contract for a pipeline crossing the property of
that company under the above circumstances, or whether the
possibility of benefit is so remote that it is not reasonably
foreseeable that there will be a material financial effect on the
North Fork Water Company. It appears that Mrs. Wright's stock in
the North Fork Water Company is worth more than $1,000.00.

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 22, 1983
Page Three

I enclose copy of the Cooperative Water Project Agreement,
which although bulky may provide some useful background
information, and copy of correspondence received from the
Operations Manager/Engineer of the District describing the
physical arrangements and the easements held by the Conservation
District.

Very truly yours,

JAMES W. DILWORTH

JWD:ckh

Enclosures

San Bernardino Valley Municipal Water District
1350 SOUTH "E" STREET • P. O. BOX 5906 • SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92412 • (714) 824-2200
(714) 889-0433

April 8, 1983


Mr. James W. Dilworth
7172 Magnolia Avenue
Riverside, CA 92504

Dear Mr. Dilworth:

     Subject:  North Fork Property for SARC Pipeline

     As you have requested, I have reviewed the effect of the proposed SARC Pipeline on the property owned by the North Fork Water Company (San Bernardino County Parcel Nos. 297-061-8 and 297-061-12). These parcels are located south of Greenspot Road near the present terminus of the Foothill Pipeline. The SARC Pipeline is proposed to traverse the property from near the northeast corner to the southwest corner.

     The SARC Pipeline will have no operational effects on the North Fork Water Company as that Company currently has water delivery facilities in-place at the "North Fork Turnout." This turnout delivers State Project water to the existing Company facilities at the North Fork Canal. The presence of the SARC Pipeline does not make water service any more available than is currently available to the property.

     Water service would not be available from this line except near Greenspot Road. Water service from the District could be made available to the North Fork Water Company from the existing Foothill line. All water service should be located near public right-of-way. Water service that could come from the SARC Pipeline is at the same pressure and conditions as that which would come from the Foothill line.

     According to the San Bernardino Valley Water Conservation District, there are blanket easements to that District for water spreading, including the right to construct, maintain, operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, etc. Copies of these easements are attached for your review.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Stephen P. Stockton
                                        Operations Manager/Engineer

T. 1 S., R. 2 W., SEBSM.

EASEMENT DEED TO CORRECT DESCRIPTION
OF PREVIOUS EASEMENT DEED

WHEREAS by Easement Deed dated May 17, 1961 and recorded
June 12, 1961 in Book 5455, Page 327, of Official Records of San Bernardino
County, California, the NORTH FORK WATER COMPANY conveyed to the SAN
BERNARDINO VALLEY WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT an easement over certain
parcels of land owned by said grantor, and

WHEREAS the description of Parcel No. 2 in said Easement Deed,
thru error and inadvertance, did not describe correctly the parcel intended
to be affected by said easement,

NOW THEREFORE, in order to correct the description of the lands
affected by said easement and particularly Parcel No. 2 described in said
deed,

FOR A GOOD CONSIDERATION, NORTH FORK WATER COMPANY, a corpora-
tion, does hereby grant to SAN BERNARDINO VALLEY WATER CONSERVATION
DISTRICT, a public agency, an easement as hereinafter described in, over,
and across those certain parcels of real property in the County of San
Bernardino, State of California, all of which are located in the North
half of Section 8 of Township 1 South, Range 2 West, San Bernardino Base
and Meridian, and which are respectively described as follows:

PARCEL NO. 1. That part of the West half of the North-
west quarter of the Northeast quarter of said Section 8,
lying South of the county road as the same now exists.

PARCEL NO. 2. That part of the South half of the North-
east quarter of the Northwest quarter of the Northeast
quarter of said Section 8, lying South of said county
road as the same now exists.

PARCEL NO. 3. The North half of the Southeast quarter
of the Northwest quarter of the Northeast quarter of said
Section 8.

PARCEL NO. 4. The Southwest quarter of the Southeast
quarter of the Northwest quarter of the Northeast quarter
of said Section 8.

REX W. CRANMER
ATTORNEY AT LAW

Said easement is for the following purposes:

(a) To cause surface waters to be diverted onto and over said
real property for the purpose of spreading such water so as to cause and
permit such water to sink underground.

(b) For use in connection with said water spreading, to con-
struct, maintain, operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, trenches,
canals and dams on said real property.

(c) For use in connection with said water spreading operations
on said real property (or elsewhere) to maintain and use one or more road-
ways on said real property for the purpose of pedestrian and vehicular
passage.

If at any time hereafter the said District shall cease to use
said real property for water spreading operations, the easements provi-
ded for herein shall forthwith terminate. Such use shall be deemed to
have terminated if water spreading is not conducted on said real property
for a period of five consecutive years.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, said corporation has executed this
instrument by and through its duly authorized officers this [illegible] day of
September, 1961.

NORTH FORK WATER COMPANY,
a corporation,

By: [illegible]

And [illegible]


STATE OF CALIFORNIA     )
                        ) ss.
COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO)

On this [illegible] day of September, 1961, before me, the undersigned,
a Notary Public in and for said County and State, personally appeared
[illegible] known to me to be the President, and
[illegible] known to me to be the Secretary of NORTH
FORK WATER COMPANY, the corporation that executed the within Easement Deed,
and acknowledged to me that such corporation executed the same.

WITNESS my hand and official seal.


                                        [illegible]
                                        Notary Public in and for said
SEAL                                    County and State
                                        [illegible]

REX W. CRAMMER
ATTORNEY AT LAW

EASEMENT DEED

FOR A GOOD CONSIDERATION, NORTH FORK WATER COMPANY,

a corporation, does hereby grant to SAN BERNARDINO VALLEY WATER CONSER-

VATION DISTRICT, a public agency, an easement as hereinafter described in,

over, and across those certain parcels of real property in the County of San

Bernardino, State of California, all of which are located in the North half

of Section 8 of Township 1 South, Range 2 West, San Bernardino Base and

Meridian, and which are respectively described as follows:

PARCEL NO. 1. That part of the West half of the North-
west quarter of the Northeast quarter of said Section 8,
lying South of the county road as the same now exists.

PARCEL NO. 2. That part of the South half of the North-
west quarter of the Northeast quarter of said Section 8,
lying South of said county road as the same now exists.

PARCEL NO. 3. The North half of the Southeast quarter
of the Northwest quarter of the Northeast quarter of said
Section 8.

PARCEL NO. 4. The Southwest quarter of the Southeast
quarter of the Northwest quarter of the Northeast quarter
of said Section 8.

Said easement is for the following purposes:

(a) To cause surface waters to be diverted onto and over said

real property for the purpose of spreading such water so as to cause and per-

mit such water to sink underground.

(b) For use in connection with said water spreading, to con-

struct, maintain, operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, trenches, canals

and dams on said real property.

(c) For use in connection with said water spreading operations

on said real property (or elsewhere) to maintain and use one or more roadways

on said real property for the purpose of pedestrian and vehicular passage.

If at any time hereafter the said District shall cease to use

said real property for water spreading operations, the easements provided


REX W. CRAMER

for foreign shall forthwith terminate. Such use shall be deemed to have
terminated if water spreading is not conducted on said real property for a
period of five consecutive years.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, said corporation has executed this
instrument by and through its duly authorized officers this 17th day of
May, 1961.

NORTH FORK WATER COMPANY,
a corporation,

By: [illegible signature]
And: [illegible signature]


STATE OF CALIFORNIA     )
                        ) ss.
COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO)

On this 19th day of May, 1961, before me, the undersigned,
a Notary Public in and for said County and State, personally appeared
[illegible] known to me to be the President, and
[illegible] known to me to be the Secretary of NORTH
FORK WATER COMPANY, the corporation that executed the within Easement
Deed, and acknowledged to me that such corporation executed the same.

WITNESS my hand and official seal.

[illegible signature]
Notary Public in and for said County
and State D H. RODDICK, Judge
Highland Judicial District
6997 Palm Ave.
Highland, California

REX W. CRAIMER

embedding.first_500_words: State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest (916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444 May 11, 1983 James Dilworth Attorney at Law 7172 Magnolia Avenue Riverside, CA 92504 Re: Advice No. A-83-118 Dear Mr. Dilworth: You have asked our advice on behalf of Margaret C. Wright, one of the elected directors of the San Bernardino Valley Municipal Water District ("District"), with respect to her participation in the proposed letting of a contract for the construction of the Santa Ana River Crossing ("SARC") Pipeline. Our advice is based on the following facts supplied in your letter seeking advice and in our subsequent telephone conversation. The SARC pipeline, which the District is planning to construct, will cross property owned by the North Fork Water Company. The North Fork property is in a floodplain, and is presently used for provision of irrigation water to its stockholders. North Fork has no plans to change the use of this property. The SARC pipeline will cross the property, but will not be used for, or available for use for, supplying water to the property now served by North Fork. The SARC pipeline will not affect the supply of water to the North Fork property. The pipeline will cross the North Fork property along an easement which has already been obtained by the District. As far as you know, the construction of the pipeline over the North Fork property will not affect the value of that property. Mrs. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in North Fork. In addition, her husband is an officer of North Fork. Mrs. Wright disqualified herself from participating in the District vote on the easement over the North Fork property. She has now asked whether she can participate in future decisions concerning the contract for the SARC pipeline. James Dilworth May 11, 1983 Page 2 The Political Reform Act prohibits an official from making or participating in a governmental decision in which she has a financial interest. Government Code Section 87100.2/ An official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on a business entity in which the official has an investment of over $1,000. Section 87103. Since Ms. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in North Fork, she must disqualify herself from participating in any decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on North Fork. From the facts that you have provided, it does not appear that the construction of the SARC pipeline over the North Fork property will have a material financial effect on the value of North Fork's property,2/ or on its income3/ or other assets. Therefore, it is
